message:
🐛 Fix active user count not updating in UI (#636)

* 🐛 Fix active user count polling permanently dying after transient failures

The useActiveUsers hook had a circuit breaker that permanently killed polling
after 3 consecutive fetch failures, with no recovery mechanism. This meant
the viewer count would freeze forever if the API was briefly unavailable.

Fixes:
- Add 30s recovery timer after circuit breaker trips instead of dying permanently
- Reset circuit breaker on manual refetch() so navigation-triggered refetches work
- Add visibilitychange listener to recover polling when tab regains focus
- Fix stale token closure in WebSocket reconnection (read fresh from localStorage)
- Always notify subscribers on first successful fetch to clear loading state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix Netlify presence race condition with per-session blob keys

The presence function stored all sessions in a single shared blob,
causing a read-modify-write race condition where concurrent heartbeats
from different tabs would overwrite each other's data. This kept the
count stuck at 1 regardless of actual viewers.

Fix: Store each session as its own blob key (session-{sessionId}).
Writes no longer conflict since each tab writes to its own key.
On GET, list all session-* blobs and count non-expired ones.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Passthrough active-users requests in MSW mock to real Netlify function

The MSW mock handler returned hardcoded { activeUsers: 1, totalConnections: 1 }
for all active-users requests, preventing the UI from ever showing the real
count on Netlify deploy previews. Use passthrough() so requests reach the
actual Netlify presence function.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🌱 Add @netlify/blobs dependency for presence function

The Netlify presence function imports @netlify/blobs but it wasn't
listed as a dependency, causing runtime errors on deploy previews.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix stuck loading state when hook mounts after initial fetch

When a second hook instance mounts after the singleton has already
fetched data, it missed the loading:false notification. Sync the
loading state from hasFetchedOnce on mount.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🌱 Regenerate package-lock.json with cross-platform support

Include linux-x64 rollup binaries so CI builds work.
Fixes: Cannot find module @rollup/rollup-linux-x64-gnu

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@
-import { http, HttpResponse, delay } from 'msw'
+import { http, HttpResponse, delay, passthrough } from 'msw'
 
 /**
  * MSW (Mock Service Worker) handlers for KubeStellar Console
@@ -235,16 +235,10 @@ export const handlers = [
     return HttpResponse.json({ status: 'ok', version: 'demo' })
   }),
 
-  // Active users (for presence tracking)
-  http.get('/api/active-users', async () => {
-    await delay(50)
-    return HttpResponse.json({ activeUsers: 1, totalConnections: 1 })
-  }),
-
-  http.post('/api/active-users', async () => {
-    await delay(50)
-    return HttpResponse.json({ success: true })
-  }),
+  // Active users (for presence tracking) — passthrough to real Netlify function
+  // so the count reflects actual connected sessions
+  http.get('/api/active-users', () => passthrough()),
+  http.post('/api/active-users', () => passthrough()),
 
   // Permissions
   http.get('/api/permissions/summary', async () => {
